dry_bulk.confluence_note: A sharp divergence exists between record-level spot rates (BDI 5y pctl 99.7%) and persistent fundamental weakness in Chinese industrial data.
dry_bulk.summary: The dry bulk market is experiencing a significant technical expansion with a 60-day ROC of +50.3%, yet Breakwave reports consistently flag negative fundamentals. High iron ore inventories in China (166mt, +20.3% YOY) and falling steel production suggest the current rate surge lacks a long-term demand foundation.
dry_bulk.outlook: Expect a correction in spot rates as momentum fades and fundamental oversupply at Chinese ports weighs on chartering activity.
dry_bulk.watch: Chinese steel production levels and iron ore inventory de-stocking trends.

tanker.confluence_note: Strong quantitative expansion is reinforced by positive fundamental sentiment arising from geopolitical disruptions and supply chain inefficiencies.
tanker.summary: Clean and dirty tankers are in a state of extreme expansion (Clean Z=+3.21) as Middle East straits remain unnavigable, forcing longer voyages. Despite a slight cooling in recent momentum, the structural shift toward alternative routing and OECD inventory draws supports a historically tight market.
tanker.outlook: Bullish conditions will likely persist so long as geopolitical tensions maintain trade route disruptions and long-haul ton-mile demand.
tanker.watch: Status of Middle East transit routes and US/China crude oil import/export arbitrage.

macro_note: Global shipping is currently bifurcated: tankers are benefiting from geopolitical chaos and supply chain friction, while dry bulk is struggling to reconcile near-record spot pricing with a slowing Chinese industrial core.
